title: rumex dentatus subsp. strictus
intro: Rumex dentatus subsp. strictus, also known as the wood sorrel, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buckwheat family and is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Rumex dentatus subsp. strictus include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic, making it a valuable herb for treating various ailments. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
